source: Manually curated, based on I4C / cybercrime.gov.in public advisories
maintainNote: Update this list yourself over time as new scam patterns emerge. No API or account needed — just edit this file and redeploy.

patterns.explanation: ‘Digital arrest’ does not exist as a legal process in India — this phrase alone is a strong scam indicator.
Messages threatening consequences tied to your Aadhaar number are a common impersonation-scam opener.
Urgent KYC-expiry threats pushing you to click a link are a widespread phishing pattern.
Fake customs/courier seizure claims are a known fraud script used to extract payment.
Fake TRAI/telecom disconnection notices are frequently used to build urgency in scam calls.
Requests to install AnyDesk or similar remote-access apps are strongly associated with fraud that drains bank accounts.
Same pattern as AnyDesk — remote-access app requests from an unsolicited caller are a major red flag.
No bank, agency, or courier service will ever legitimately ask for your OTP.
Scammers often stage a fake uniformed video call to appear like real law enforcement.
Threats of immediate legal action over a loan/EMI are a common recovery-scam pressure tactic.
Fake courier calls claiming your parcel contains illegal items are a well-documented scam script.
Urgent SIM-block threats are commonly used to pressure victims into sharing personal details.
Fake income-tax notices sent via SMS/WhatsApp with a payment link are a known phishing tactic.
Generic urgent account-suspension threats are a broad phishing pattern across many scam scripts.
Claims of contact from CBI over phone/video call, especially demanding money, are a hallmark of digital-arrest scams.
